items.title: Scientists finally solved how a common gut bacterium triggers colon cancer
items.summary: Researchers solved a long-standing mystery behind how a bacterial toxin associated with colorectal cancer damages the colon. items.title: More than 400 sick as CDC searches for the source of a mystery outbreak
items.summary: A growing Cyclospora outbreak has sickened more than 400 people in four states, and investigators are still searching for the contaminated food responsible. The CDC warns the actual number of cases is likely much higher and urges anyone with symptoms to seek medical care.

items.title: Common constipation drug may help clear depression brain fog
items.summary: An existing constipation drug may have an unexpected new use: helping clear the "brain fog" that often lingers after depression. items.title: The hidden skeleton “gatekeeper” inside brain cells could help fight Alzheimer's
items.summary: Researchers have discovered that a microscopic skeleton inside neurons does much more than hold cells together. items.title: This pet gecko could help scientists unlock the secrets of cancer
items.summary: An unusual leopard gecko that naturally develops aggressive tumors may become an important new model for cancer research. Scientists found its tumors share key genetic changes with human cancers, offering a rare opportunity to study the disease as it develops naturally.
